context: Evaluating the x-research MCP server for use in social listening and competitive intelligence workflows. Key questions: Does search return structured, useful data? How expensive are user timeline pulls? Are engagement metrics accessible on the free/Basic API tier? Can we chain user lookup -> tweets -> engagement reliably? Run via /eval-capture since this is a stdio server, not HTTP.

tests.name: search-topic-ai-agents
tests.workflow_step: Search for recent tweets on a business-relevant topic to assess content quality and structure
assertions: Results are relevant to AI agents topic

tests.name: get-user-by-username
tests.workflow_step: Look up a known account to get numeric ID for downstream calls

tests.name: get-user-tweets-original
tests.workflow_step: Pull original tweets from the looked-up user to measure timeline token cost and content structure
assertions: Response is useful for competitive intelligence on company messaging

tests.name: get-user-mentions
tests.workflow_step: Get mentions to assess public conversation and sentiment
assertions: Each result has author info separate from mentioned user
Results show what people are saying about the account

tests.name: get-trends-denmark
tests.workflow_step: Get Danish trending topics for local market intelligence
assertions: Tweet volume data present for at least some trends

tests.name: get-tweet-retweeters
tests.workflow_step: Get retweeters of a specific tweet to test engagement data access on the current API tier
assertions: Returns data or clear API tier error
If successful, retweeter profiles include usernames
